summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

For f(x) = {2x, x<1; x+3, x‚Č•1}, what is f(1)?

Since 1 ‚Č• 1, use the second piece: f(1) = 1 + 3 = 4.

For the same function, what is lim(x‚Üí1‚ĀĽ) f(x)?

From the left (x < 1): f(x) = 2x ‚Üí 2(1) = 2.

For g(x) = {x¬≤, x‚Č§0; sin x, x>0}, what is lim(x‚Üí0) g(x)?

lim(x‚Üí0‚ĀĽ) x¬≤ = 0 and lim(x‚Üí0‚Āļ) sin x = 0. Both equal 0, so the limit is 0.

For f(x) = {3, x<‚ąí1; x¬≤, ‚ąí1‚Č§x‚Č§2; 5, x>2}, what is f(‚ąí1)?

Since ‚ąí1 is in [‚ąí1, 2], use f(x) = x¬≤: f(‚ąí1) = (‚ąí1)¬≤ = 1.

For h(x) = {(x¬≤‚ąí4)/(x‚ąí2), x‚Č†2; 5, x=2}, what is lim(x‚Üí2) h(x)?

For x ‚Č† 2: (x¬≤‚ąí4)/(x‚ąí2) = x+2. lim(x‚Üí2) = 4. The limit is 4, though h(2) = 5.

What is a 'piecewise function'?

A piecewise function uses different formulas on different sub-intervals, joined at boundary points. It may or may not be continuous.

What is a 'Heaviside step function'?

The Heaviside step function jumps from 0 to 1 at x = 0. It models on/off switches in engineering.

What is a 'spline'?

A spline is a piecewise polynomial where not only the function but also its derivatives match at boundary points, ensuring smooth connections.

What is a 'removable discontinuity' in a piecewise function?

A removable discontinuity can be 'repaired' by redefining the function value to equal the limit at that point.

A tax system uses T(x) = {0.10x, x‚Č§20000; 2000+0.20(x‚ąí20000), x>20000}. What is the tax on $25,000?

T(25000) = 2000 + 0.20(25000 ‚ąí 20000) = 2000 + 0.20(5000) = 2000 + 1000 = $3,000.

A medication dosage model is D(t) = {100(1‚ąíe^(‚ąít)), 0‚Č§t<8; D‚āÄ¬∑e^(‚ąí0.5(t‚ąí8)), t‚Č•8} where D‚āÄ makes D continuous at t = 8. What is D‚āÄ?

For continuity at t = 8: D‚āÄ¬∑e‚Āį = 100(1‚ąíe‚ĀĽ‚Āł). So D‚āÄ = 100(1‚ąíe‚ĀĽ‚Āł) ‚Čą 99.97.

A robot's velocity changes according to v(t) = {2t, 0‚Č§t<5; 10, 5‚Č§t<10; 10‚ąí(t‚ąí10), t‚Č•10}. What is the velocity at t = 12?

For t ‚Č• 10: v(12) = 10 ‚ąí (12 ‚ąí 10) = 10 ‚ąí 2 = 8 m/s.

A temperature controller uses T(t) = {20+5t, 0‚Č§t<4; 40, 4‚Č§t<8; 40‚ąí3(t‚ąí8), t‚Č•8}. What is the temperature at t = 10?

For t ‚Č• 8: T(10) = 40 ‚ąí 3(10 ‚ąí 8) = 40 ‚ąí 6 = 34¬įC.

summary_sections.title: Calculation Methods
summary_sections.content_html:
How to Calculate

Example: Find a so that f(x) = {ax+1, x<3; 2x‚ąí2, x‚Č•3} is continuous at x = 3.

Solution: Continuity: a(3) + 1 = 2(3) ‚ąí 2 ‚Üí 3a + 1 = 4 ‚Üí 3a = 3 ‚Üí a = 1.

Example: For f(x) = {x¬≤‚ąí1, x<1; k, x=1; 2x‚ąí1, x>1}, find k so that f is continuous at x = 1.

Solution: lim(x‚Üí1‚ĀĽ) = 1‚ąí1 = 0, lim(x‚Üí1‚Āļ) = 2‚ąí1 = 1. Since 0 ‚Č† 1, no value of k makes f continuous.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A medication dosage model is D(t) = {100(1‚ąíe^(‚ąít)), 0‚Č§t<8; D‚āÄ¬∑e^(‚ąí0.5(t‚ąí8)), t‚Č•8} where D‚āÄ makes D continuous at t = 8. What is D‚āÄ?

For continuity at t = 8: D‚āÄ¬∑e‚Āį = 100(1‚ąíe‚ĀĽ‚Āł). So D‚āÄ = 100(1‚ąíe‚ĀĽ‚Āł) ‚Čą 99.97.
